title: Workfare Skills Support (WSS) (Basic)
text:
Workfare Skills Support (WSS) (Basic) - SupportGoWhere
Workfare Skills Support (WSS) (Basic)
Share link
Provides training allowance and cash incentives to encourage lower-wage Singaporean workers to upskill via training.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Provides training allowance for trainees who self-sponsor their own short training
Provides cash award for trainees who complete sustained training
Provides absentee payroll for employers who send eligible employees for training
No application required for employees; self-employed persons and employers should submit claims online
Collapse all sections
About this support

Training Allowance (TA): $6 per training hour (from 1 July 2026: $10.50 per training hour) for self-sponsored trainees

Training commitment award: Up to $1,000 (from 1 July 2026: up to $800) for achieving the required certifications

Absentee payroll for employers sending eligible employees for training: 95% trainee’s basic hourly wage, capped at $13 per training hour

WSS (Basic) supports short training, such as:

Workplace Literacy and Numeracy courses

Recommended courses that meet Progressive Wage Model (PWM) training requirements

Who is this for?

Singapore Citizen;

At least 30 years old or older as of 31 December of the work year; or at least 13 years old for persons with disabilities.

Earn an average gross monthly income of not more than $3,000 for the past 12 months;

Have declared your net trade income and made MediSave contributions in full if you are self-employed;

Live in a property with an annual value of $21,000 and below as at 31 December of the preceding year;

Own not more than one property; and

If you are married:

you and your spouse together not own more than one property; and

the assessable income of your spouse does not exceed $70,000 of the preceding Year of Assessment

 

The above is similar to the Workfare Income Supplement (WIS) criteria.

How to apply?

Employees

No application needed.

 

Self-employed persons

You will need to provide in the TA claim form within 120 days of course end date:

softcopy of your proof of self-employment (for example, contract for service or equivalent dated within 3 months after course end date, or ACRA business registration and business transaction documents dated within 3 months after course end date)

your course details (for example, course title, course start and end dates, and training provider’s name




Employers

You can apply online at Enterprise Portal for Jobs and Skills

Where can I find help?

View more details

WSS hotline: 1800 536 8333

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 19 May 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
